steps.title: Identify the subject and its grammatical number
steps.content: The subject is the noun phrase “Each of these republics.” The word “each” is a singular determiner — the subject is singular regardless of the plural noun that follows. The blank requires an auxiliary verb (a form of “be”) to accompany the past participle “established.”

steps.title: Apply the subject-verb agreement rule
steps.content: ”was” (singular, past) provides a singular verb for the singular subject “each” and past tense to match the historical context (“was the capital,” “spoke Mari”). The other choices are either plural (have been, are, were) or present (are), violating subject-verb agreement or tense consistency. That matches choice D — D is correct.
